message:
feat(robot): enhance agent mail integration and add markdown section filtering

This commit improves the robot output mode with better Agent Mail integration
and more granular control over markdown output.

Agent Mail Integration Enhancements (robot.go):
- Fixed error message to use client.BaseURL() instead of hardcoded DefaultBaseURL
- Added thread tracking: messages are now grouped by thread_id, with unique
  thread count reported in ThreadsKnown field
- Added pane mapping: Agent Mail identities are matched to tmux panes by
  exact title match, enabling PendingMail hints per agent in snapshot output
- Excluded "HumanOverseer" from agent iteration (not a real agent)
- Moved AgentMail snapshot building earlier so PendingMail can be attached
  to agents during snapshot construction
- Added Pane field to SnapshotAgentMailStats for cross-referencing

InboxMessage Type Enhancement (agentmail/types.go):
- Added ThreadID field (*string) to capture thread associations in inbox messages

New Robot-Markdown CLI Flags (root.go):
- --md-sections: Comma-separated list of sections to include (sessions, beads,
  alerts, mail) for selective output
- --md-max-beads: Override maximum beads shown per category
- --md-max-alerts: Override maximum alerts shown

These flags enable AI orchestrators to request only relevant sections,
reducing token usage in LLM contexts.

Also included in this commit:
- JSON output support for `ntm config show` command when --json flag is used
- Outputs structured config including palette, agents, tmux, checkpoints,
  and alerts settings

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -62,6 +62,7 @@ type InboxMessage struct {
 	Subject     string    `json:"subject"`
 	From        string    `json:"from"`
 	CreatedTS   time.Time `json:"created_ts"`
+	ThreadID    *string   `json:"thread_id,omitempty"`
 	Importance  string    `json:"importance"`
 	AckRequired bool      `json:"ack_required"`
 	Kind        string    `json:"kind"`
